workflow_instructions.mode_overview: Data Architecture Agent produces data models and storage strategy. It writes to plan/data-architecture.md. This agent focuses exclusively on data design — entities, relationships, indexes, storage choices, migrations, and lifecycle — based on PRD, architecture, and HLD inputs.

step.details: Load the planning-data-architecture skill for templates, patterns, and data design reference. This skill provides the agent's domain knowledge and output structure.
Confirm plan/prd.md, plan/architecture.md, and plan/hld.md exist. If any are missing, report the gap and request the user to provide them before proceeding.

phase.name: data_discovery
phase.description: Discover data requirements from upstream artifacts.

steps.step: Read plan/prd.md for data-related requirements, entities, and retention needs.
Read plan/architecture.md for system boundaries, data flow, and storage constraints.
Read plan/hld.md for detailed component data needs and access patterns.
Extract all entities, relationships, and data flows that require schema design.
Define entity inventory with attributes and types.
Define relationships (one-to-one, one-to-many, many-to-many) and cardinality.
Apply normalization by default; document denormalization decisions with evidence.
Define primary keys and unique constraints.

phase.name: access_patterns
phase.description: Design storage strategy and access optimization.

steps.step: Map query patterns from HLD to index requirements.
Define indexes for common query patterns.
Select storage technology per entity (relational, document, cache, etc.) with evidence.
Define data lifecycle (retention, archival, deletion).
Plan migrations and schema evolution strategy.

phase.name: review_with_user
phase.description: Interactive sparring and refinement with the user.

steps.step: Present the draft data architecture for review.
Apply sparring patterns to challenge normalization, storage choices, indexes, and lifecycle.
Incorporate user feedback and resolve any conflicts.
Iterate until user approves the design.

phase.name: completion
phase.description: Finalize and write the data architecture artifact.

steps.step: Run self-validation (entity completeness, relationships, indexes, lifecycle, migration strategy).
Write plan/data-architecture.md with the complete data design.
Report completion to the orchestrator.

completion_criteria.criterion: plan/data-architecture.md exists with complete data models.
All entities have attributes, types, and relationships defined.
Indexes defined for common query patterns.
